description.text: If the company details or structure have changed contact us on {telephone_number_link} or email {email_link}

list: Data should relate to all sites in the UK. This includes England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland.
We will treat your information securely and confidentially.

This survey is refreshed every two weeks. To avoid losing data and to help us provide real-time information please submit by the return by date, or at the latest before the next fortnightly survey goes live.
On average it takes 5 minutes to complete this survey once you have collected the information.
You can save a copy of your answers at the end of this questionnaire using the ’Download as PDF’ button.

contents.description: Please complete this survey even if you are a sole trader and it does not seem relevant to your business. All returns are important, as it allows decision-makers to understand how businesses of all sizes are performing in the current climate.
Thank you for participating in this important survey.
View the questions before you start the survey (this link opens in a new tab)
This survey has the following sections:
This section asks for the business’s trading status.
This section asks for information about your workforce such as:
This section asks for information about:

list: types of contract used in your business

contents.description: This section asks for information about:

list: your business’s use of cloud computing services
your business’s spending on cloud computing services
rights to exclusive use of data

contents.description: This section asks about:

list: your business’s concerns regarding energy prices
your business’s concerns regarding increases in fuel prices

contents.description: This section asks about:

list: factors impacting your business’s supply chains

contents.description: This section asks for information about:
This section asks for information about the impact of US tariff changes on your business

The data from this survey is used widely to enable rapid response, and inform policy decisions about issues impacting UK businesses and the economy. This includes topics relating to business resilience, financial performance, trade and your workforce.
View the latest publication produced from this survey (this link opens in a new tab)

blocks.page_title: Trading status of your business section

contents.description: This section asks for the business’s trading status.

title.text: Which of the following statements best describes {ru_name}’s trading status?

description:
You can interpret ’trading’ as ’operating’.

Only select ’paused trading’ if all trading or operations have temporarily stopped.

options.label: Currently partially trading
options.description: For example, trading with reduced hours or staff numbers

options.label: Paused trading but intends to restart in the next two weeks
Paused trading and does not intend to restart in the next two weeks

contents.description: This section asks for information about your workforce such as:

title.text: Is {ru_name} using, or intending to use, increased homeworking as a permanent business model going forward?

description: If you are a sole trader and do not have a workforce, please base your answers on your own working arrangement.

question.title: Why is your business using, or intending to use, increased homeworking as a permanent business model going forward?

options.label: Ability to better match jobs to skills
Ability to recruit from a wider geographical pool in the UK
Ability to recruit from a wider geographical pool internationally
Reduced carbon emissions
options.description: For example, due to less travelling to and from the workplace

question.title: Why is your business not intending to use increased homeworking as a permanent business model going forward?

options.label: Concerns with integrating new starters into the workplace
Costs of employee equipment
options.description: For example, the technology and equipment required to enable homeworking such as laptops or desks

options.label: Reduced reliability and retention of staff

question.title: Which, if any, of the following skills has your business had a high demand for in the last 12 months?

options.label: Advanced digital skills
options.description: For example, skills in data analysis, coding, artificial intelligence, cyber security , or software development.

options.label: Basic digital skills
options.description: For example, using emails, spreadsheets, online forms, business software, video calls, or other everyday digital tools.

options.label: Customer service skills
options.description: For example, communicating with customers, handling queries or complaints, supporting sales, or improving customer experiences.

options.label: Management or leadership skills
options.description: For example, managing staff, leading teams, planning work, making decisions, or supporting employee development.

options.label: Manual skills
options.description: For example, practical or hands-on skills used to operate, repair, build, install, manufacture, maintain, or handle goods, equipment, or materials.

options.label: Transferable skills
options.description: For example, communication, problem solving, teamwork, adaptability, and organisation skills.

question.title: Which of the following skills, if any, do you expect your business to have a high demand for over the next 12 months?

options.label: Advanced digital skills
options.description: For example, skills in data analysis, coding, artificial intelligence, cyber security , or software development.

options.label: Basic digital skills
options.description: For example, using emails, spreadsheets, online forms, business software, video calls, or other everyday digital tools.

options.label: Customer service skills
options.description: For example, communicating with customers, handling queries or complaints, supporting sales, or improving customer experiences.

options.label: Management or leadership skills
options.description: For example, managing staff, leading teams, planning work, making decisions, or supporting employee development.

options.label: Manual skills
options.description: For example, practical or hands-on skills used to operate, repair, build, install, manufacture, maintain, or handle goods, equipment, or materials.

options.label: Transferable skills
options.description: For example, communication, problem solving, teamwork, adaptability, and organisation skills.

options.label: Business does not expect a high demand of skills over the next 12 months

blocks.page_title: Skills demand over the next 12 months

question.title: Which, if any, of the following skills does your workforce require extra support or training in?

options.label: Advanced digital skills
options.description: For example, skills in data analysis, coding, artificial intelligence, cyber security , or software development.

options.label: Basic digital skills
options.description: For example, using emails, spreadsheets, online forms, business software, video calls, or other everyday digital tools.

options.label: Customer service skills
options.description: For example, communicating with customers, handling queries or complaints, supporting sales, or improving customer experiences.

options.label: Management or leadership skills
options.description: For example, managing staff, leading teams, planning work, making decisions, or supporting employee development.

options.label: Manual skills
options.description: For example, practical or hands-on skills used to operate, repair, build, install, manufacture, maintain, or handle goods, equipment, or materials.

options.label: Transferable skills
options.description: For example, communication, problem solving, teamwork, adaptability, and organisation skills.

blocks.page_title: Extra support or training for the workforce

question.title: What impact is not meeting skills demand likely to have on your business’s growth over the next 12 months?

definitions.title: What we mean by ’not meeting skills demand’

contents.description: This refers to gaps between the skills employers require and the skills available in the workforce.

options.label: Delays to expanding into new markets or locations
options.description: For example, difficulty entering new markets, serving new customers, or opening new sites because of skills gaps.

options.label: Delays to expanding into new products or services
options.description: For example, difficulty developing, launching, or delivering new products or services because of skills gaps.

options.label: Reduced ability to adopt new technologies, processes or innovate
options.description: For example, difficulty introducing new technology, improving ways of working, or developing new ideas because of skills gaps.

options.label: No impact on the growth of the business

blocks.page_title: Impact of not meeting skills demand

question.title: Which types of training, if any, does your business provide to employees?

options.label: Customer service or sales training
options.description: For example, training focused on customer interaction, service delivery or sales activities

options.label: Digital or IT skills training
options.description: For example, training in software, IT systems, coding, or other digital tools

options.label: Job specific training
options.description: For example, training to develop skills specific to an employee’s job or occupation

options.label: Management or leadership training
options.description: For example, training related to managing people, teams or organisational leadership

options.label: Training to support the introduction of new technologies, equipment or processes
options.description: For example, training provided specifically to implement new systems, equipment or ways of working.

options.label: Transferable skills training
options.description: For example, training in communication, teamwork, problem solving

options.label: Business does not provide training beyond mandatory requirements

blocks.page_title: Types of training provided to employees

question.title: Which of the following, if any, are barriers to your business providing more training to employees?

options.label: Cost
options.description: For example, direct financial costs such as fees or materials.

options.label: Difficulty finding suitable training or training providers
options.description: For example, training that covers the required skills, is at the appropriate level, or can be delivered by a suitable local provider

options.label: Lack of information about training opportunities
options.description: For example, difficulty identifying what training is available or which options would be more suitable.

options.label: Lack of staff time
options.description: For example, difficulty releasing employees from their usual work to take part in training.

options.label: Staff are already fully proficient
options.description: For example, employees already have the required skills.

options.label: Unclear return on investment
options.description: For example, uncertainty about whether training would improve performance, productivity or retention.

options.label: Business has no barriers to providing more training to employees

blocks.page_title: Barriers to business providing more the training to employees

question.title: On average, how did employees’ hourly wages in April 2026 compare with employees’ hourly wages in the previous calendar month?
description: If you are a sole trader and do not have a workforce, please base your answers on your own working arrangement.

question.title: How have your business’s staffing costs changed over the last three months?

contents.description: Include:
Exclude:

question.title: How does your business expect staffing costs to change over the next three months?

contents.description: Include:
Exclude:

question.title: How does your business plan to adapt to any future increases in employment costs?

options.label: Reduce employee benefits
options.description: This refers to non-statutory perks or compensation that are provided to employees in addition to their base salaries and wages

options.label: Reduce wage increases for other employees

question.title: Is your business currently experiencing a shortage of workers?
Which of the following challenges, if any, has your business experienced due to a shortage of workers?

options.label: Business had to pause trading entirely
Business had to recruit temporary workers
Had to pause trading of some of the business
Business has not experienced any challenges due to a shortage of workers

question.title: Did your business experience any difficulties recruiting employees in April 2026?
Which, if any, of the following employees has your business had difficulties recruiting in April 2026?

options.label: Clerical employees
options.description: For example, payroll clerks and secretaries

options.label: Professional or managerial employees
options.description: For example, accountants, lawyers and sales managers

options.label: Semi-skilled or unskilled employees
options.description: For example, bartenders, retail salespersons and security guards

options.label: Skilled, manual or technical employees
options.description: For example, carpenters, electricians and plumbers

options.label: Business did not experience any difficulties in recruiting employees

blocks.page_title: Type of employees business had difficulties in recruiting

question.title: Why did your business experience difficulties recruiting employees in April 2026?

options.label: Business cannot offer an attractive pay package to applicants
options.description: For example, additional benefits, competitive pay and flexible working hours

options.label: Increased number of vacancies
options.description: For example, business growth and diversification, or new work opportunities

options.label: Lack of qualified applicants for the roles on offer
options.description: For example, applicants lacking the experience, qualifications or skills required

options.label: Low number of applications for the roles on offer
Reduced number of applicants aged 16 to 24 years
Reduced number of applicants aged over 50 years

blocks.page_title: Reasons for difficulties faced in recruiting employees

contents.description: This section asks for information about:

list: types of contract used in your business

title.text: Which of the following contract arrangements, if any, does {ru_name} currently use for workers?

contents.description: This refers to:

list: people directly employed by the business
agency workers not directly employed by the business

options.label: Contracts with more than 24 hours guaranteed per week
Contracts greater than 16 and up to 24 hours guaranteed per week
Contracts greater than 8 and up to 16 hours guaranteed per week
Contracts with 1 to 8 hours guaranteed per week
Workers on agency contracts
options.description: For example, not directly contracted by business

options.label: Zero hours contract
options.description: For example, no guaranteed minimum hours

options.label: Business does not have any contracted workers

question.title: In the last 12 months, has your business had substitution clauses in its contracts?

contents.description: Exclude substitution clauses that would be considered ineffectual. For example, if there is an oral contract between the parties that the worker must undertake the work personally, the written clause allowing the right of substitution may not be genuine and may be ignored as ineffectual.
This refers to a clause which explicitly states that the contractor can use someone else to complete the work.

question.title: In the last 12 months, approximately what percentage of contractors have used substitution clauses to complete their work?

contents.description: Exclude substitution clauses that would be considered ineffectual. For example, if there is an oral contract between the parties that the worker must undertake the work personally, the written clause allowing the right of substitution may not be genuine and may be ignored as ineffectual.
This refers to a clause which explicitly states that the contractor can use someone else to complete the work.

blocks.page_title: Percentage of contractors using substitution clauses to complete work

list: your business’s use of cloud computing services
your business’s spending on cloud computing services
rights to exclusive use of data

title.text: Which of the following, if any, does {ru_name} use cloud computing services for?

definitions.title: What we mean by ’cloud computing services’

contents.description: This refers to services or software accessed over the internet, rather than installed on a personal computer or on site business servers.

options.label: E-mail tools
options.description: For example, e-mail platforms or messaging tools

options.label: Enterprise resource planning (ERP) or business management software
options.description: For example, HR, supply chain or finance systems

options.label: Networking or IT infrastructure services
options.description: For example, cloud networking

options.label: Office or productivity software
options.description: For example, word processing or spreadsheets

options.label: Business does not use cloud computing services

question.title: Does your business pay for access to cloud based software?

options.label: Yes, paid on a short term basis
options.description: For example, monthly or less than one year

options.label: Yes, paid on a longer term basis
options.description: For example, one year or more

options.label: Business does not pay to use any cloud based software

blocks.page_title: Payment for access to cloud based software

question.title: For accounting purposes, how does your business usually record spending on cloud based software that is used for more than one year?

options.label: Recorded as an ongoing operating cost
options.description: For example, expensed in the year the cost is incurred

options.label: Recorded as an investment or asset
options.description: For example, capitalised and spread over more than one year

blocks.page_title: Business record spending on cloud based software

question.title: In the last 12 months, has your business purchased data from an external organisation?

contents.description: Include paid access to datasets, databases and data services
Exclude software and data storing or hosting

options.label: Business has not purchased data from external organisation

blocks.page_title: Data purchased from an external organisation

question.title: At the time of purchase, was your business the only organisation with the right to use the data?

options.label: Yes, the business had exclusive rights
No, the same data could be sold or licensed to others

blocks.page_title: Exclusive right to use purchased data

contents.description: This section asks about:

list: your business’s concerns regarding energy prices
your business’s concerns regarding increases in fuel prices

title.text: How concerned is {ru_name} about energy prices?

question.title: How concerned is your business about increases in fuel prices?

contents.description: This refers to costs associated with petrol and diesel used in business operations, including for transportation, machinery, and equipment.

blocks.page_title: Business concern about increases in fuel prices

contents.description: This section asks about:

list: factors impacting your business’s supply chains

title.text: Which of the following factors, if any, is {ru_name} concerned about impacting supply chains over the next 12 months?

options.label: Increased barriers to trade
options.description: This refers to barriers that make it more difficult for businesses to source the inputs they are seeking from a given market. These can include tariff increases and new regulations.

options.label: Market distorting practices
options.description: This refers to practices that may undermine free and fair trade and prevent businesses from competing based on merit

options.label: Nature and biodiversity risks
options.description: This refers to the negative impacts businesses may face if, for example, the environment is damaged or species are lost

options.label: Shipping disruption
options.description: For example, the Red Sea crisis

options.label: Business is not concerned about factors impacting supply chains over the next 12 months

blocks.page_title: Factors impacting supply chains in next 12 months

question.title: How does your business expect these factors to impact supply chains?

options.label: Shortage of inputs
options.description: This refers to any resources used to create goods and services. These include intermediate goods used in production, labour, fuel, and equipment.

options.label: Business does not expect these factors to impact supply chains

question.title: Which of the following actions, if any, did your business take to increase supply chain resilience in the last 12 months?

definitions.title: What we mean by ’increase supply chain resilience’

contents.description: This refers to the ability of the business to respond to disruptions and reduces the likelihood and scale of any future disruptions.

options.label: Demand management
options.description: For example, identifying whether it is beneficial to manage the demand for components or goods, considering substitutes and alternatives, innovation, and circularity

options.label: Diversification of suppliers across a broader range of countries
Increased awareness of supply chains
options.description: For example, greater data gathering and sharing

options.label: Onshoring
options.description: This refers to sourcing inputs from the company’s original country

options.label: Relocated existing sourcing to another country

blocks.page_title: Actions taken to increase supply chain resilience

question.title: Which of the following actions, if any, does your business plan to take to increase supply chain resilience over the next 12 months?

definitions.title: What we mean by ’increase supply chain resilience’

contents.description: This refers to the ability of the business to respond to disruptions and reduces the likelihood and scale of any future disruptions.

options.label: Demand management
options.description: For example, identifying whether it is beneficial to manage the demand for components or goods, considering substitutes and alternatives, innovation, and circularity

options.label: Diversification of suppliers across a broader range of countries
Increase awareness of supply chains
options.description: For example, greater data gathering and sharing

options.label: Onshoring
options.description: This refers to sourcing inputs from the company’s original country

options.label: Relocate existing sourcing to another country

blocks.page_title: Planned actions to increase supply chain resilience

contents.description: This section asks for information about:

title.text: Has {ru_name} exported goods or services in the last 12 months?

contents.description: Include exporting services from your businesses in the UK to an individual or business located outside of the UK
This refers to skills and services that are provided by people and are ’intangible’.
For example, your business may provide a service, such as legal or financial services, to a non-UK business.

options.label: Business has not exported goods or services in the last 12 months

question.title: Has your business imported goods or services in the last 12 months?

contents.description: Include importing services from an individual or business located outside of the UK to your businesses in the UK
This refers to skills and services that are provided by people and are ’intangible’.
For example, your business may receive a service, such as research or construction, from a non-UK business.

options.label: Business has not imported goods or services in the last 12 months

contents.description: This section asks for information about the impact of US tariff changes on your business

title.text: What impact, if any, did the United States tariffs have on {ru_name} in the last month?

contents.description: Include impacts from other countries in your supply chain that are being affected by the United States tariffs

options.label: Business passed additional costs to customers
Business stopped or paused exports to the United States

blocks.page_title: Impact of US tariffs in the last month

question.title: What impact, if any, do you expect the United States tariffs to have on your business in the next month?

contents.description: Include impacts from other countries in your supply chain that are being affected by the United States tariffs

options.label: Business will explore new export markets
Business will pass additional costs to customers
Business will stop or pause exports to the United States
No impact expected for the business

blocks.page_title: Impact of US tariffs in the next month

title.text: Which of the following occupational groups best represents your position at {ru_name}?

options.label: Administrative and secretarial occupations
options.description: For example, administrative, secretarial and related occupations

options.label: Associate professional occupations
options.description: For example, science, engineering and technology associate professionals, health and social care associate professionals, protective service occupations, culture, media and sports occupations, business and public service associate professionals

options.label: Caring, leisure and other service occupations
options.description: For example, caring personal service occupations, leisure, travel and related personal service occupations and community and civil enforcement occupations

options.label: Elementary occupations
options.description: For example, elementary trades and related occupations and elementary administration and service occupations

options.label: Managers, directors and senior officials
options.description: For example, corporate managers and directors or other managers and proprietors

options.label: Process, plant and machine operatives
options.description: For example, process, plant and machine operatives and transport and mobile machine drivers and operatives

options.label: Professional occupations
options.description: For example, science, research, engineering and technology professionals, health professionals, teaching and other educational professionals and business, media and public service professionals

options.label: Sales and customer service occupations
options.description: For example, sales and customer service occupations

options.label: Skilled trades occupations
options.description: For example, skilled agricultural and related trades, skilled metal, electrical and electronic trades, skilled construction and building trades and textiles, printing and other skilled trades

guidance.show_guidance: Why are we asking this question?
guidance.hide_guidance: Why are we asking this question?

contents.description: Your answer provides us with a better understanding of the data collected by this survey.

blocks.page_title: Occupation of person completing this survey

question.title: Please tell us anything else about your business’s current situation relating to topics covered in this survey
